intro: Gilibertia grandiflora, also known as New Zealand flax or harakeke, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the Asparagaceae family. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine for its numerous health benefits, including wound healing, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Gilibertia grandiflora include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Gilibertia grandiflora is commonly used in herbal preparations such as creams, ointments, and tinctures, which are used topically to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other inflammatory disorders.
